title: Iran/Iraq, UN Security Council Assessing Violations of International Humanitarian Law
author_or_org: ICRC Online Casebook summarizing 1983 UN mission
pages_or_sections: Abadan section (May 23, 1983), lines 251-262; Khorramshahr section (May 23, 1983), lines 267-273
geographic_scope: Iranian urban war zones including Abadan and Khorramshahr
date_scope: Observations and authority-reported war damage up to May 1983, with specific recollection of September-November 1980 fighting
strengths: Conflict-specific field mission with city-level descriptions, prewar populations, evacuation notes, and short casualty statements.
limitations: Relies partly on host-government briefings; some figures are qualitative or cumulative, and military losses are thin.
notes: Most useful for Khorramshahr civilian deaths and for Abadan depopulation/siege context.

citation_short: Cordesman, Lessons of Modern War, ch. 4 (1990)
title: The Lessons of Modern War, Volume II: The Iran-Iraq War, Chapter 4: Phase One: Iraq's Invasion of Iran
strengths: Operationally detailed narrative with explicit Iraqi killed and wounded at Khorramshahr, Iranian defender counts, and linked Abadan force levels.
limitations: Some totals are mixed ('killed and seriously wounded'); attacker force sizes are partly unit-structure descriptions rather than exact headcounts.
notes: Best single source for Khorramshahr military-loss context in this bundle.

title: ĀBĀDĀN ii. The modern city
strengths: Provides city-proper census history and a clean 1976 population anchor.
limitations: City proper only; does not include the broader suburban figure reported by the 1983 mission.
notes: Useful low anchor for Abadan population denominators.

title: ĀBĀDĀN iii. Basic Population Data, 1956-2011
strengths: Confirms war-period depopulation and the city's zero count in the 1986 national census.
notes: Supports sustained evacuation/depopulation after the first wave of attacks.

title: KHORRAMSHAHR i. PHYSICAL AND HUMAN GEOGRAPHY
strengths: Provides 1976 city population and confirms destruction/abandonment after the Iraqi attack.
limitations: Not a casualty source; city proper only.
notes: Key low anchor for Khorramshahr's civilian denominator.

title: KHORRAMSHAHR ii. POPULATION, 1956-2011
strengths: Confirms evacuation and zero population in the 1986 census after destruction.
notes: Used as an evacuation/depopulation anchor for the Khorramshahr civilian denominator.

citation_short: Cordesman, Lessons of Modern War, ch. 7 (1990)
title: The Lessons of Modern War, Volume II: The Iran-Iraq War, Chapter 7: Phase Four: Stalemate and War of Attrition on the Land and Escalation of the War in the Gulf
geographic_scope: Hawizeh Marshes, Qurnah-Uzayr corridor, Majnoon approaches
strengths: Operationally specific on force sizes and sequence of counterattacks in Operation Badr.
limitations: Battle losses are mostly reported as 'men lost' or casualties rather than clean direct deaths; civilian exposure footprint is thin.
notes: Useful for a holdout attrition episode but insufficient for a clean core DR.

citation_short: Khaji et al., all Iranian cities missile casualties (2010)
title: Civilian casualties of Iranian cities by ballistic missile attacks during the Iraq-Iran war (1980-1988)
author_or_org: Ali Khaji, Shoaodin Fallahdoost, Mohammad Reza Soroush
strengths: Uses an army-staff database built from daily wartime reports and clearly separates deaths from injuries.
limitations: Civilian-only series; not city-by-city in the abstract, and not matched to military exposures for DR.
notes: Best aggregate civilian numerator series for Iraqi ballistic missile attacks on Iranian cities.

citation_short: Khaji et al., Tehran missile casualties (2012)
title: Civilian casualties of Iraqi ballistic missile attack to Tehran, capital of Iran
author_or_org: Ali Khaji, Shoaodin Fallahdoost, Mohammad Reza Soroush, Vafa Rahimi Movaghar, Song Shuang-ming
source_type: peer-reviewed medical article / open-access journal abstract
strengths: Highly specific on time window, number of missiles, civilian deaths, injuries, and partial sex breakdown.
limitations: No matched military death/exposure series for Tehran; female-death figure is for identified cases only.
notes: Strong single-city numerator source for the 1988 Tehran missile phase.

citation_short: Cordesman, Lessons of Modern War, ch. 13 (1990)
title: The Lessons of Modern War, Volume II: The Iran-Iraq War, Chapter 13: The Air and Missile Wars and Weapons of Mass Destruction
geographic_scope: Iranian and Iraqi cities; missile war generally
strengths: Places the 1988 missile campaign in operational and political context and gives displacement indicators.
limitations: Sparse on exact city-level civilian death counts compared with Khaji's studies.
notes: Useful mainly for context and displacement rather than for precise death numerators.

title: Iraq Missile Overview
geographic_scope: Tehran and broader Iran-Iraq missile war
strengths: Compact summary of the seven-week Baghdad-Tehran missile exchange and the scale of flight from Tehran.
limitations: Secondary synthesis rather than primary casualty registry; rounded numbers.
notes: Best used as contextual corroboration, not as the primary casualty source.

title: Saddam's War: An Iraqi Military Perspective of the Iran-Iraq War
author_or_org: Kevin M. Woods, Williamson Murray, Thomas Holaday / Institute for Defense Analyses
strengths: Independent Iraqi-perspective retrospective that highlights the battle's severity.
limitations: Reports casualties rather than clean direct deaths; retrospective and interview-based.
notes: Used to show why Khorramshahr loss definitions should not be averaged mechanically across sources.

title: United Nations Iran-Iraq Military Observer Group (UNIIMOG) background
strengths: Authoritative statement of the ceasefire date.
limitations: Not episode-specific and not a casualty source.
notes: Used only to support the conflict end date of 1988-08-20.
